description: When a theorem's statement mentions a concrete named object (E2, π, a specific group, etc.) but its PROOF after one or two rewrites never mentions that object again - the proof works purely with abstract properties (q-expansion, summability, polynomial bound, ...) - both /generalise and /mathlibable must flag this as YES-but-generalise-first with reason 'proof-independent of concrete witness'. The adversarial diagnostic: (1) count occurrences of the named-object identifier in the proof body (excluding the statement line); (2) if after the FIRST rw [<unfolding>] the object identifier vanishes - STOP and propose the abstract version. Add as: /mathlibable Phase 4c Question 8: 'Concrete-via-abstract: does the proof use only abstract properties the named object happens to satisfy?'; /generalise Phase 5 mandatory inversion check: 'Is THIS theorem a corollary of a more abstract theorem whose proof is identical?' (not just 'can hypotheses be weakened?'). Evidence: mathlib4#40765 isBoundedAtImInfty_E2 - reviewer flagged 'general statement with q-expansion + polynomially bounded coefficients + no negative-exponent terms is bounded at i∞; deduce E2 from that'. Both skills returned 'maximally general/already idiomatic'. Proof confirms: after rw [E2_eq_tsum_cexp], identifier E2 never appears again.
